title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: main radiatR vignette covers path raw tracking files plotted set headings. vignette picks heading data frame shows analysis layer: Dispersion summaries — circ_dispersion(), sector_summary() Parametric fits — vonmises_fit(), wrappedcauchy_fit() Hypothesis tests — test_uniformity(), test_mean_directions(), test_concentration(), multiple-comparison correction Correlation — circ_cor() (circular-linear circular-circular) Distribution overlays — add_angle_rose(), add_vonmises_density(), add_wrappedcauchy_density(), add_circular_kde() Significance geometry — add_critical_r() (Rayleigh / V-test circle) add_critical_v_line() (V-test boundary) Every statistics function takes data frame heading column radians (default name "heading") returns tidy data frame, results drop straight dplyr, knitr::kable(), plotting.

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: derive one heading per trial bundled cpunctatus dataset ring-crossing rule, attach trial’s target half-width (arc) grouping. construction used main vignette. heading column radians, reference-relative (0 = toward target). Everything operates column. need summary heading frame , circ_summary_headings() derives headings tracks chosen rule returns circular summary one call. group_by = NULL pools trials; group_by = "id" (default) gives one row per trial:
code: data(cpunctatus)  hd <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "crossing",                       circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4,                       coords = "relative",                       angle_convention = "clock") #> Warning: derive_headings(rule = 'crossing'): 25 of 251 trials (10.0%) produced #> no heading and are excluded from circular statistics. Rule-based failures are #> often non-random and can bias results; inspect attr(x, "missing_ids"). names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id"  # attach target half-width (arc) from the dataset, by trial arc_map  <- unique(cpunctatus@data[, c("trial_id", "arc")]) hd       <- merge(hd, arc_map, by = "trial_id") hd$arc   <- factor(hd$arc)  # keep trials with a defined crossing heading hd <- hd[is.finite(hd$heading), , drop = FALSE] head(hd[, c("trial_id", "arc", "heading")]) #>   trial_id arc   heading #> 1   10_1_1  10 0.2777794 #> 2  10_10_1  10 3.0376774 #> 3  10_11_1  10 6.0710737 #> 4  10_12_1  10 2.2074837 #> 5  10_13_1  10 0.9322361 #> 6  10_14_1  10 4.7955587 circ_summary_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "distal", group_by = NULL) #>   mean_dir resultant_R      kappa   n group #> 1 1.814938  0.04215183 0.08437866 235   all

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: circ_dispersion() returns mean direction, resultant length R, circular standard deviation. Grouping arc gives one row per condition. R runs 0 (uniformly scattered) 1 (headings identical); circular SD moves opposite way. dense per-frame heading series — gaze direction tethered subject, say — sector_summary() bins angles reports dwell proportions per sector:

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: vonmises_fit() estimates mean direction μ\mu concentration κ\kappa maximum likelihood, asymptotic standard errors confidence interval μ\mu: wrappedcauchy_fit() heavier-tailed alternative — robust data outliers weak directionality. concentration ρ\rho bounded [0,1)[0, 1): return row per group, quick merge() puts two concentration estimates side side comparison.

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: test_uniformity() asks, per group, whether headings preferred direction. Rayleigh test gives exact p-value; testing many conditions , pass p_adjust corrected p_value_adj column. multimodal non-symmetric alternatives Rayleigh low power, hermans_rasson pycke omnibus tests (test = "hermans_rasson" / test = "pycke") powerful, cost Monte-Carlo p-value:

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: test_mean_directions() Watson-Williams test — circular analogue one-way ANOVA mean angle. omnibus form asks whether group differs: Set pairwise = TRUE pairwise comparisons; p_adjust strongly recommended number comparisons grows quickly:
code: test_mean_directions(hd, group_col = "arc") #>   n_groups statistic df1 df2     p_value            test #> 1        8  8.230315   7 218 6.70879e-09 Watson-Williams pw <- test_mean_directions(hd, group_col = "arc",                            pairwise = TRUE, p_adjust = "holm") head(pw[order(pw$p_value_adj), ]) #>    group1 group2 statistic df1 df2      p_value            test  p_value_adj #> 14     20     30  68.71016   1  56 2.590906e-11 Watson-Williams 7.254536e-10 #> 22     30      0  45.35938   1  65 5.086470e-09 Watson-Williams 1.373347e-07 #> 6      10     50  31.92401   1  57 5.341214e-07 Watson-Williams 1.388716e-05 #> 4      10     40  24.34873   1  52 8.658990e-06 Watson-Williams 2.164747e-04 #> 1      10     15  16.68434   1  60 1.328071e-04 Watson-Williams 3.187369e-03 #> 13     15      0  16.32869   1  58 1.586887e-04 Watson-Williams 3.649841e-03

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: test_concentration() checks whether groups equally concentrated (circular analogue test equal variances), key assumption behind Watson-Williams test :
code: test_concentration(hd, group_col = "arc") #>   statistic df    p_value        test #> 1  14.23259  7 0.04719587 equal.kappa

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: circ_model_select() ranks five candidate distributions AICc – uniform, unimodal, axial, unimodal_uniform (directed mode uniform background), bimodal (asymmetric two-mode mixture) – reports Akaike weights. answers whether heading sample best described undirected, singly directed, axially oriented, directed--scatter, genuinely bimodal. five models mirror Schnute-Groot family implemented CircMLE package (Fitak & Johnsen 2017); radiatR fits natively (two mixtures numerical maximum likelihood) uses human-readable labels rather M1-M5 codes.

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: circ_cor() measures association headings covariate. x_type = "linear" (default) computes circular-linear correlation — , whether heading direction associated numeric target half-width: returned r unsigned (association strength, 0–1); test statistic nr2n r^2 approximately χ22\chi^2_2. two angular variables, pass x_type = "circular" get Fisher’s ρ∈[−1,1]\rho \[-1, 1].
code: hd$arc_num <- as.numeric(as.character(hd$arc)) circ_cor(hd, x_col = "arc_num", angle_col = "heading", x_type = "linear") #>           r   n            type statistic df    p_value #> 1 0.2175965 226 circular-linear   10.7007  2 0.00474648

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: circ_cor() measures strength association, circ_regression() models : fits Fisher–Lee circular–linear regression heading one linear covariates, via formula interface circular::lm.circular(). show fit recovers known effect, simulate trajectories whose mean heading shifts predictor — per-condition mean_slope controls shift — fit model back: coefficient table recovers positive, significant slope predictor, confirming simulated effect. Note magnitude: Fisher–Lee model links mean heading linear predictor 2arctan⁡(⋅)2\arctan(\cdot) function, fitted slope link scale attenuated relative plain mean_slope used simulate. Interpret sign significance coefficients directly, use predict() read effect back angular (heading) scale: predicted mean heading tracks predictor — rotating value — exactly simulation set . fitted relationship can drawn straight onto circular panel: covariate value becomes mean-direction arrow, colour-graded predictor, see mean heading sweep covariate increases.
code: cond <- data.frame(condition = "demo", n_trials = 120, ref_mean = 0,                    concentration_base = 12, mean_slope = 0.6,                    predictor_mean = 0, predictor_sd = 1) s   <- simulate_tracks(conditions = cond, n_points = 8, seed = 1) reg <- s[!duplicated(s$trial_id), c("predictor", "final_heading")] names(reg)[2] <- "heading"  fit <- circ_regression(reg, heading ~ predictor) summary(fit) #>        term  estimate         se statistic p_value  conf.low conf.high #> 1 predictor 0.3281699 0.02007503  16.34717       0 0.2888236 0.3675163 predict(fit, data.frame(predictor = c(-1, 0, 1))) #> [1] 5.6202847 6.2544774 0.6054847 radiate(headings_frame(hd, heading, units = "radians")) +   add_circ_mean(fitted_directions(fit, at = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 7)),                 colour_col = "predictor")

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: overlay layers draw angular distribution Cartesian unit-disc space radiate(), compose onto bare circular canvas +. build canvas : rose diagram bins headings wedges; parametric non-parametric density curves overlay top. Giving every layer scale aligns radii, shapes can compared directly:  grey wedges empirical rose; steelblue curve von Mises fit, darkorange wrapped Cauchy, tomato circular kernel density estimate. parametric curves track rose closely fit good; systematic gap (especially tails) cue prefer heavier-tailed wrapped Cauchy. show individual headings rather smoothed distribution, add_stacked_headings() fans coincident headings radial columns dots, reducing overplotting points stacked angle. needs headings_frame (object derive_headings() returns), use fresh one — hd demoted plain data frame merge()/subset:  step sets gap dots start_sep inward offset first dot rim; pass axial = TRUE draw datum poles.
code: canvas <- ggplot() + coord_fixed() +   add_circ(radius = 1) +   theme_void() vm <- vonmises_fit(hd)        # pooled fit (group_col = NULL) wc <- wrappedcauchy_fit(hd)  canvas +   add_angle_rose(hd, bins = 12, scale = 0.8, fill = "grey80") +   add_circular_kde(hd, scale = 0.8, colour = "tomato") +   add_vonmises_density(vm, scale = 0.8, colour = "steelblue") +   add_wrappedcauchy_density(wc, scale = 0.8, colour = "darkorange") hd_frame <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "distal") radiate(hd_frame, show_markers = FALSE) + add_stacked_headings(hd_frame)

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: Heading-summary overlays subdivide column draw one element per group, coloured – handy comparing conditions one plot. mean-direction arrow (add_heading_arrow()) takes colour_col; bootstrap confidence interval (add_heading_interval()) takes group_col. split target half-width (arc):  arc group gets mean vector CI arc matching colour. grouping independent faceting, can (example) colour cohort faceting radiate() treatment. radiate()’s built-arrow follows idea via arrow_colour_col, drawing one arrow per group:
code: canvas +   add_heading_arrow(hd,    colour_col = "arc") +   add_heading_interval(hd, group_col  = "arc", stat = "bootstrap_ci") radiate(cpunctatus, group_col = "trial_id",         show_arrow = TRUE, arrow_colour_col = "arc")

title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: remaining two helpers draw decision boundary significance test directly resultant-length space (radius 0–1), can read observed mean vector. add_critical_r() draws Rayleigh critical circle: mean resultant length needed significance level alpha, namely rcrit=−log⁡(α)/nr_\text{crit} = \sqrt{-\log(\alpha)/n}. mean vector reaches past circle, headings significantly non-uniform.  arrow tip lies well outside dashed critical circle, matching tiny Rayleigh p-value . add_critical_v_line() corresponding boundary V-test, tests uniformity specified direction mu0 (0, .e. toward target). Unlike Rayleigh circle, V-test boundary straight line perpendicular mu0: significance requires mean vector’s projection onto mu0 exceed c=zα/2nc = z_\alpha / \sqrt{2n}. Set show_region = TRUE shade rejection side.  experiment priori expected direction (target), V-test powerful omnibus Rayleigh test, line makes one-sided nature decision visible.
code: disp <- circ_dispersion(hd) mean_vec <- data.frame(x = disp$resultant_R * cos(disp$mean_dir),                        y = disp$resultant_R * sin(disp$mean_dir))  canvas +   add_critical_r(hd, alpha = 0.05, test = "rayleigh") +   geom_segment(data = mean_vec,                aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = x, yend = y),                arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "inches")),                linewidth = 1) test_uniformity(hd, test = "rayleigh") #>   statistic    p_value   n     test #> 1 0.1298181 0.02217652 226 rayleigh canvas +   add_critical_v_line(hd, mu0 = 0, alpha = 0.05, show_region = TRUE) +   geom_segment(data = mean_vec,                aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = x, yend = y),                arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "inches")),                linewidth = 1)

what: Periodic data: time of day or year
title: Circular Statistics and Distribution Overlays
text: Headings angles. periodic variable — time day event occurs (chronobiology), day year (phenology), generic cycle — maps onto circle, every tool applies. as_angle() mapping clock/calendar convention circumference labels, 06:00 event lands "6" o’clock label.  dots cluster clock positions, Rayleigh test confirms day uniform. Use period = "year" Date/POSIXct day--year data (pair angle_labels = "months"), positive number custom cycle length.
code: set.seed(1) # 200 events: a strong morning cluster (~09:00) and a weaker evening one (~21:00) times <- as.POSIXct("2020-06-01", tz = "UTC") +   c(rnorm(150, 9 * 3600, 1.2 * 3600), rnorm(50, 21 * 3600, 3600))  events <- headings_frame(data.frame(heading = as_angle(times, "day")),                          col = heading, units = "radians")  # is the timing non-uniform over the day? test_uniformity(events, test = "rayleigh") #>   statistic      p_value   n     test #> 1 0.4789747 1.183343e-20 200 rayleigh  radiate(events, angle_labels = "hours")

title: Working with Loaders
text: radiatR reads tabular (CSV/TSV) exports many tracking tools named dialects. dialect maps tool’s column conventions onto Tracks model one position per individual per frame. Pass dialect name read_tracks(path, dialect = "name"); tool-specific options go dialect_args. table summarises currently handled. Two limitations general: Tabular text . Dialects read CSV/TSV (, optional packages installed, JSON/XML/Parquet). Native binary formats — NumPy .npz MATLAB .mat — read except noted (ctrax reads .mat via R.matlab package). Position, posture. radiatR models single position per individual per frame. Per-frame posture data (variable-length midline outline polylines) outside model. tool tracks discrete body keypoints, can loaded extra columns turned body-axis heading bodypart_axis rule pose_to_headings(); dense posture polylines ingested. tool’s export directly supported, two escape hatches remain: convert tidy CSV use read_tracks() explicit mapping, register custom dialect (see Registering Custom Dialect ).

Dialects live -memory current session. persist across projects, place registration call package .Rprofile source using radiatR.
code: fake_json <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") jsonlite::write_json(   list(     tracks = list(       A = list(timestamp = c(0, 1, 2), px = c(0, 1, 2), py = c(0, 0.2, 0.5)),       B = list(timestamp = c(0, 1, 2), px = c(1, 1.3, 1.5), py = c(0, -0.1, -0.2))     )   ),   fake_json,   auto_unbox = TRUE )  json_tracks_fn <- function(x) {   dat <- if (is.character(x) && file.exists(x)) jsonlite::fromJSON(x) else x   entries <- purrr::imap(dat$tracks, function(track, id) {     data.frame(       id   = id,       time = track$timestamp,       x    = track$px,       y    = track$py,       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   })   do.call(rbind, entries) } register_loader_dialect("json_tracks", json_tracks_fn) ts_json <- read_tracks(json_tracks_fn(fake_json)) ts_json #> Tracks: 2 trajectories, 6 observations #> Columns: id='id', time='time', angle='angle' (radians), x='x', y='y' #>     id time   x    y     angle #> A.1  A    0 0.0  0.0 0.0000000 #> A.2  A    1 1.0  0.2 0.1973956 #> A.3  A    2 2.0  0.5 0.2449787 #> B.1  B    0 1.0  0.0 0.0000000 #> B.2  B    1 1.3 -0.1 6.2064134 #> B.3  B    2 1.5 -0.2 6.1506338

Angles can supplied directly, reconstructed movement trajectories. package includes: utilities importing paired landmark/track text files produced popular tracking suites; helpers extracting trial limits computing circular summary statistics; polished ggplot2 layers drawing concentric guides annotated paths. Prefer point--click interface? launch_app() opens interactive Shiny companion upload tracks headings, configure analysis, view figures together radiatR code reproduces .

title: radiatR
text: package ships full set example tracks (millipede orientation experiment) demonstrate import workflow. Landmark files (_point01.txt) record two pixel-space reference points per trial (origin target location circumference); track files (_point02.txt) contain full xy trajectory. get_all_object_pos() reads landmark/track pair, normalises coordinates unit circle (radius = 1), returns Tracks. Trial metadata (physical radius, target position, frame limits) ts_demo@meta$trial_limits.

title: radiatR
text: radiate() draws trajectories unit circle concentric reference rings. Colouring arc factor shows paths cluster condition.  default radiate() plots landmark-relative frame — trajectory rotated trial landmark (e.g. stimulus) sits direction. inspect un-rotated frame experimental control (instance, check apparatus carries directional bias ), pass coords = "absolute":  circumference can labelled domain units instead degrees — useful time--year compass data. angle_labels selects built-scale aligns tick count :  built-scales "cardinal" (compass), "hours" (24-hour clock), "seconds". finer control — 8-point compass, 12-hour clock, different month formats — add explicit circumference_labs() layer built scale_cardinal(), scale_clock(), scale_months(), scale_seconds():  n_labels sets many equally-spaced ticks numeric labels draw — e.g. 12 clock face, 4 cardinals , 0 bare circle — angle_label_position places numeric labels "outside" (default), "inside", "split" (cardinals inside, diagonals outside):

title: radiatR
text: Attach capture frame rate (frames per second) Tracks object time aspect frame-indexed tracks can reported real seconds shown plot. frame rate stored object’s metadata; time/frame column never altered. track_duration() reports elapsed seconds trajectory, track_colour = "time" colours path elapsed time (POSIXct time works without frame rate).  frame rate also lets track_speed() report trajectory’s speed real units. default unit-circle coordinates radii per second. frame rate lets radiate() colour path instantaneous speed, per-observation sibling track_speed().  know physical scale, calibrate distances lengths speeds report real units. scale physical units per coordinate unit (e.g. mm per radius); unset, everything stays coordinate units (radii). frame rate also unlocks per-observation kinematics: velocity_vector() gives velocity components (vx, vy; distance-calibrated scale set) angular_velocity() signed turning rate (counter-clockwise positive). velocity_angle() gives direction travel observation, convention headings — feeds straight circular tools: Reduced one row per track, track_velocity() gives path’s net (average) velocity vector track_turning() summarises turning rate. non-circular view, plot_profile() draws per-observation kinematics metric — instantaneous speed turning rate — elapsed time, one line per track. ggplot sibling radiate().   metric = "direction" plots movement direction time (points, since direction circular):  plot_speed_direction() crosses two — observation’s speed movement direction. speed axis robustly clipped default single-frame tracking artifacts crush display:  pooled speed distribution, median CV, comes plot_speed_histogram():

title: radiatR
text: crossing method — projecting vector two concentric-ring crossings unit circle — assigns one directional heading per trial. derive_headings() return_coords = TRUE returns heading angle inner-ring crossing position. Overlaying heading endpoints (one hollow circle per trial) grand mean direction combined trajectory plot gives compact summary full dataset:  grand mean arrow points 283.2° relative reference direction (clock convention; 0° = toward reference) R = 0.08, reflecting overall reference-relative tendency across conditions.
code: hd <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "crossing",                       circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4,                       return_coords = TRUE) #> Warning: derive_headings(rule = 'crossing'): 25 of 251 trials (10.0%) produced #> no heading and are excluded from circular statistics. Rule-based failures are #> often non-random and can bias results; inspect attr(x, "missing_ids"). names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id" # join the target half-width (arc) from the dataset for grouping/faceting arc_map <- unique(cpunctatus@data[, c("trial_id", "arc")]) hd       <- merge(hd, arc_map, by = "trial_id") hd$arc   <- factor(hd$arc) attr(hd, "colour_col") <- "arc" attr(hd, "display")    <- circ_display(zero = 0) head(hd[, c("trial_id", "arc", "heading", "x_inner", "y_inner")]) #>   trial_id arc   heading     x_inner     y_inner #> 1   10_1_1  10 0.3545811  0.19556350 -0.04102813 #> 2  10_10_1  10 6.2337825 -0.03715972  0.19558015 #> 3  10_11_1  10 1.3353505 -0.07231344  0.18624466 #> 4  10_12_1  10 2.2846471 -0.18291981  0.08084798 #> 5  10_13_1  10 5.6521348  0.18223038  0.08232129 #> 6  10_14_1  10 1.6589765  0.09025066  0.17836615 p_all <- radiate(cpunctatus,                  group_col   = "trial_id",                  colour_col  = "arc",                  show_labels = FALSE,                  show_arrow  = FALSE,                  display     = circ_display(zero = 0)) +   add_heading_points(hd, colour_col = "arc", size = 1, alpha = 0.6)  p_all + add_heading_arrow(hd) #> Warning: Removed 105 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_path()`). #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: radiatR
text: add_circular_boxplot() overlays Tukey-like boxplot circular data (Buttarazzi, Pandolfo & Porzio, 2018): box spans central 50 % around circular median, whiskers reach concentration-adjusted fence, far-values marked individually. composes onto radiate() plot like overlay helpers.  circ_boxplot_stats() returns underlying numeric summary — circular median, box hinges, fences, far-values, closed-form von Mises fence multiplier — boxplot can inspected reproduced independently plot: boxplot recommended uniform multimodal data: clear central direction box spans roughly half circle summary uninformative. circ_boxplot_stats() flags cases via drawable reason fields, add_circular_boxplot() emits warning rather drawing misleading box. Position-based axial data supported via axial = TRUE, draws box whiskers poles median axis.

title: radiatR
text: Three strategies control trajectories coloured. Option 1 — single colour. Pass colour string directly heading-overlay helpers; omit colour_col radiate() draw tracks default grey. Option 2 — cycling palette. colour_cycle assigns trajectory colour index cycles back 1 every n trajectories. facets set cycle restarts independently within panel trajectory 1 every panel always gets colour 1. Pass integer automatic palette assignment, character vector specify colours explicitly. Option 3 — variable mapping. colour_col maps data column colour (e.g. arc factor used combined-trajectory plot ).

title: radiatR
text: Faceting arc angle shows condition separately. Within panel, assign_cycle_colours() distinguishes individual trajectories cycling 10 colours, resetting panel boundary. Calling explicitly track data headings data frame (joining trial id) means heading markers inherit exact per-trajectory colour — single per-condition colour — colour_col used .  two-way layout, rows cols facet grid via ggplot2::facet_grid() (mirroring ggplot — takes one columns): Use rows/cols (grid) facets (wrap), . + Bootstrap CI arc added first sits behind heading markers:  + Heading vectors (dotted lines inner crossing circumference):  + Heading points top CI arc:   panel shows trajectories heading markers coloured per-trajectory cycling palette, bootstrap CI arc radius 1.05 condition colour (rendered behind heading points), solid mean direction arrow, degree labels. clock convention (0° = toward reference, clockwise). Use strip_position = "inside" place label inside plot area, strip_labels = FALSE suppress .

<- assign_cycle_colours(cpunctatus@data,                                             id_col    = "trial_id",                                             n         = 10, attr(hd_cc, "display")    <- attr(hd, "display", exact = TRUE) attr(hd_cc, "colour_col") <- "cycle_colour" p <- radiate(cpunctatus_cc,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "cycle_colour",         facets     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE,         display      = circ_display(zero = 0)) p #> Warning: Removed group_col = "arc", colour = "black",                                stat = "bootstrap_ci", boot_reps = 999L) p #> Warning: Removed 105 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range title: radiatR
text: Three functions handle directional density overlays; designed compose cleanly density source: Three built-methods available compute_circular_density(): "vonmises" (MLE via circular::mle.vonmises()), "kernel" (circular KDE), "histogram" (bin counts). computation rendering steps separate, density column can replaced values model — including Bayesian posterior predictive density brms — calling add_circular_density(): convenience wrapper add_heading_density() skips intermediate step substitution needed. combined panel plot uses shade per-condition von Mises density alongside heading vectors:  shaded region von Mises density fitted crossing headings within arc condition. narrower, taller peak indicates concentrated directional responses. Switch method = "kernel" non-parametric estimate, method = "histogram" raw count display.
code: dens_df <- compute_circular_density(hd, colour_col = "arc", method = "vonmises") # replace with Bayesian posterior: dens_df$density <- my_brms_fitted_density(dens_df$theta) add_circular_density(dens_df, colour_col = "arc", fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35) radiate(cpunctatus_cc,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "cycle_colour",         facets     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE,         display      = circ_display(zero = 0)) +   add_heading_density(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc",                       method = "vonmises", scale = 0.4,                       fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35) +   add_heading_vectors(hd_cc) +   add_heading_arrow(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black") #> Warning: Removed 105 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_path()`). #> Warning: Removed 25 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`).

title: radiatR
text: compute_circular_density() boot_reps > 0 runs non-parametric bootstrap: heading samples drawn replacement, von Mises MLE fitted replicate, density re-evaluated angular grid. boot_alpha / 2 1 - boot_alpha / 2 quantiles across replicates become density_lower density_upper columns, add_circular_density() renders shaded band around fitted curve.  darker band 95% bootstrap confidence interval fitted von Mises density. wider band reflects greater parametric uncertainty, typically seen smaller diffuse conditions. density_lower density_upper columns dens_boot can replaced interval values Bayesian model (e.g. 2.5th 97.5th percentiles brms posterior predictive distribution) passing add_circular_density().
code: dens_boot <- compute_circular_density(hd, colour_col = "arc",                                       method = "vonmises",                                       boot_reps = 499L, boot_alpha = 0.05,                                       n_theta = 300L)  radiate(cpunctatus,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         facets     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE,         display      = circ_display(zero = 0)) +   add_circular_density(dens_boot, colour_col = "arc",                        scale = 0.4, fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35,                        ci_fill = "grey60", ci_alpha = 0.35) +   add_heading_arrow(hd, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black") #> Warning: Removed 105 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_path()`).

title: radiatR
text: derive_headings() supports fourteen built-rules. "crossing" well suited echinoderm-style tracks — moderate tortuosity, consistent outward movement — rules may appropriate depending taxon experimental design. Use list_heading_rules() see available names; custom rules can added register_heading_rule(). Two parameter-free alternatives especially useful:

Per-condition resultant lengths "distal" close "crossing", consistent millipede tracks fairly direct: Visualising distal headings per-condition panel confirms spatial pattern consistent crossing method:  millipede tracks nearly radial, heading estimates agree closely across methods. sinuous species (moths, pigeons) choice rule larger influence result.

text: net rule returns direction vector first recorded position last, ignoring intermediate points. fastest possible estimate natural match GPS vanishing-bearing studies release final observed positions available. tracks three methods give similar resultant lengths. title: Simulating Trajectories
text: simulate_tracks() generates synthetic trajectories unit disk without tracking files. main uses : Verifying new analysis pipeline works real data arrives. Reproducing plots examples documentation. Teaching: demonstrating concentration, tortuosity, directional bias affect appearance paths. Informal power analysis: generate data plausible effect size check whether expected statistical pattern visible.

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Called arguments, simulate_tracks() returns tibble three conditions (60 trials total, 200 frames ): row one frame. key columns :
code: sim <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1) dim(sim) #> [1] 12000    24 names(sim) #>  [1] "condition"     "trial_id"      "trial_num"     "frame"         #>  [5] "predictor"     "concentration" "tortuosity"    "ref_heading"   #>  [9] "final_heading" "rho"           "abs_theta"     "rel_theta"     #> [13] "abs_x"         "abs_y"         "rel_x"         "rel_y"         #> [17] "modality"      "n_modes"       "mode_id"       "mode_mean"     #> [21] "track_shape"   "n_reversals"   "amplitude"     "line_width"

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: output argument controls returned. output = "trajset" path wraps tibble Tracks (absolute coordinates, normalisation). Use whenever want plug straight plotting analysis functions.
code: # Default: long-form tibble sim_tbl  <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "tibble")  # Tracks directly (ready for radiate(), derive_headings(), etc.) sim_ts   <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "trajset")  # Both representations in a named list sim_both <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "both") names(sim_both)  # "tibble" "trajset"

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: three default conditions differ directional bias, concentration, tortuosity (see ).
code: ts_default <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "trajset") radiate(ts_default,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         facets     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: simulation behaviour controlled data frame passed conditions. conditions = NULL function fills three-condition template. Supply data frame override subset columns; missing columns receive sensible defaults.

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: concentration_base von Mises κ passed rvonmises(). Higher values produce tightly clustered headings; values near zero produce near-uniform heading distributions.  κ = 1 mean arrow short (low resultant length); κ = 12 nearly trials point direction.
code: conds_kappa <- data.frame(   condition          = c("low (κ=0.1)", "medium (κ=1)", "high (κ=12)"),   n_trials           = 20L,   concentration_base = c(0.1, 1, 12),   tortuosity_base    = 0.05 ) ts_kappa <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_kappa, seed = 42,                              output = "trajset") radiate(ts_kappa,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         facets     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Two parameters control within-trial path shape: tortuosity_base — SD per-step angular noise. Larger values produce sinuous paths. phi — autocorrelation successive angular deviations (0 = white noise, values near 1 produce smooth, sweeping curves). Passed directly simulate_tracks(), via conditions table.
code: conds_tort <- data.frame(   condition          = c("smooth (φ=0.95)", "moderate (φ=0.7)", "noisy (φ=0)"),   n_trials           = 15L,   concentration_base = 6,   tortuosity_base    = c(0.12, 0.12, 0.12) ) phi_vals <- c(0.95, 0.7, 0)  # Simulate each phi separately and bind parts <- lapply(seq_along(phi_vals), function(i) {   d <- conds_tort[i, , drop = FALSE]   simulate_tracks(conditions = d, n_points = 200,                   phi = phi_vals[i], seed = 7 + i) }) ts_tort <- tracks(   do.call(rbind, parts),   id = "trial_id", time = "frame",   x = "abs_x", y = "abs_y", angle = "abs_theta",   angle_unit = "radians", normalize_xy = FALSE ) radiate(ts_tort,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         facets     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: ref_mean shifts reference direction away East (0 rad). Use simulate experiments reference along positive x-axis.
code: conds_bias <- data.frame(   condition          = c("East (0°)", "North (90°)", "Northwest (135°)"),   n_trials           = 20L,   concentration_base = 5,   tortuosity_base    = 0.06,   ref_mean          = c(0, pi / 2, 3 * pi / 4) ) ts_bias <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_bias, seed = 99,                            output = "trajset") radiate(ts_bias,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         facets     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: common design monotone gradient cue strength. example simulates four levels increasing concentration decreasing tortuosity — mimicking orientation experiment stronger cues produce tighter, smoother paths.
code: conds_grad <- data.frame(   condition          = paste0(c(15, 30, 60, 120), "° arc"),   n_trials           = 25L,   concentration_base = c(1.5, 3, 6, 10),   tortuosity_base    = c(0.14, 0.10, 0.06, 0.03),   tortuosity_sd      = 0.015 ) ts_grad <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_grad, n_points = 200,                             seed = 55, output = "trajset") radiate(ts_grad,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "condition",         facets     = "condition",         ncol         = 4,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: concentration_slope tortuosity_slope non-zero, final kappa tortuosity trial depend sampled predictor value. lets model experiments individual differences (e.g. body size, prior exposure) modulate response. Plotting concentration predictor confirms linear relationship:
code: conds_pred <- data.frame(   condition            = "gradient",   n_trials             = 40L,   concentration_base   = 3,   concentration_slope  = 4,    # kappa rises with predictor   tortuosity_base      = 0.10,   tortuosity_slope     = -0.04, # smoother at high predictor   predictor_mean       = 0,   predictor_sd         = 1 ) sim_pred <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_pred, seed = 7, output = "both")  # Each trial gets its own predictor sample range(sim_pred$tibble$predictor) #> [1] -1.387996  2.716752 range(sim_pred$tibble$concentration) #> [1]  0.10000 13.86701 trial_summary <- unique(sim_pred$tibble[, c("trial_id", "predictor",                                              "concentration")]) ggplot(trial_summary, aes(predictor, concentration)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "steelblue") +   labs(x = "Predictor", y = "Effective κ") +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Simulated Tracks objects feed directly derive_headings() circ_summarise(), making straightforward run analysis pipeline synthetic data. Visualise headings alongside trajectories:
code: conds_analysis <- data.frame(   condition          = c("diffuse", "concentrated"),   n_trials           = 30L,   concentration_base = c(1.5, 8),   tortuosity_base    = c(0.12, 0.04) ) sim_analysis <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_analysis, seed = 21,                                  output = "both")  hd <- derive_headings(sim_analysis$trajset, rule = "crossing",                        circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4,                        coords = "absolute",                        angle_convention = "unit_circle",                        return_coords = TRUE) names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id"  # derive_headings returns only id/heading/coords; join condition back cond_map <- unique(sim_analysis$tibble[, c("trial_id", "condition")]) hd <- merge(hd, cond_map, by = "trial_id")  compute_circ_mean(hd, group_col = "condition")[,   c("condition", "mean_dir", "resultant_R")] #>      condition   mean_dir resultant_R #> 1 concentrated 0.05301978   0.9039690 #> 2      diffuse 0.29012285   0.7131608 p <- radiate(sim_analysis$trajset,              group_col    = "trial_id",              colour_col   = "condition",              facets     = "condition",              ncol         = 2,              show_labels  = FALSE,              show_arrow   = FALSE) +   add_heading_points(hd, colour_col = "condition", size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +   add_heading_arrow(hd, colour_col = "condition", colour = "black") p

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: simulate_tracks() can also attach known directional ground truth, handy sanity-checking circular statistics: know analysis recover. modality condition sets per-trial principal headings distributed — uniform (preferred direction), unimodal (one von Mises mode), axial (two antipodal modes), multimodal (n_modes evenly spaced modes). one condition per modality, fed directional heading rule (net) circ_model_select(), recovers generating model: unimodal axial conditions picked cleanly (Akaike weight near 1); uniform condition leaves uniform unimodal near-tie, preferred direction fit. track_shape condition sets within-track geometry instead. track_shape = "oscillatory" condition produces back--forth tracks along principal axis — bidirectional pattern per-track axial methods built . oscillatory tracks form genuinely axial position cloud, position-based estimator pca_axis recovers axis default sampling density, directional rule (net) sees --fro directions cancel: oscillatory line-width (line_width) fixed fraction amplitude, independent tortuosity_base, axis recoverable default settings used without signal--noise tuning. Note position-based methods (pca_axis, ransac_straight) robust choice: step-based velocity_axis recovers axis coarse sampling, per-step axial signal shrinks n_points perpendicular jitter step , dense sampling lets jitter flip estimate toward perpendicular.
code: conds <- tibble::tibble(   condition          = c("uniform", "unimodal", "axial"),   n_trials           = 60L,   ref_mean           = 0.4,   concentration_base = 6,   modality           = c("uniform", "unimodal", "axial") ) sim <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds, n_points = 80,                        output = "both", seed = 7)  # net heading per trial, with the condition label joined back on hd <- derive_headings(sim$trajset, rule = "net") names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id" hd <- merge(hd, unique(sim$tibble[, c("trial_id", "condition")]),             by = "trial_id")  # best-supported model per condition (AICc; lowest dAICc = best) ms <- circ_model_select(hd, group_col = "condition") do.call(rbind, lapply(split(ms, ms$condition), function(d) {   d[which.min(d$AICc), c("condition", "model", "weight")] })) #>          condition    model    weight #> axial        axial    axial 0.8243673 #> uniform    uniform unimodal 0.3306096 #> unimodal  unimodal unimodal 0.7256195 osc <- tibble::tibble(condition = "osc", n_trials = 40L, ref_mean = 0.6,                       concentration_base = 50, track_shape = "oscillatory",                       n_reversals = 4L) ts_osc <- simulate_tracks(conditions = osc, output = "trajset", seed = 11)  # pca_axis recovers the ~0.6 rad axis (read it as an axial mean) at defaults pa <- derive_headings(ts_osc, rule = "pca_axis") circ_summarise(pa, "heading", axial = TRUE, units = "radians",                stats = "mean_dir") #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   mean_dir #>      <dbl> #> 1    0.611  # net cannot: the directions cancel to a tiny resultant length net <- derive_headings(ts_osc, rule = "net") circ_summarise(net, "heading", units = "radians", stats = "resultant_R") #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   resultant_R #>         <dbl> #> 1       0.152

title: Quantifying path shape
text: Alongside circular heading statistics (see vignette("circular-statistics")), radiatR provides spatial metrics describe geometry trajectory: far travelled, straight , tortuous. per-track scalars, returned tidy data frames keyed trajectory id, computed recorded x/y coordinates. two complementary notions tortuosity: Displacement-based — straightness_index() / tortuosity_ratio() — compare net start--end displacement total distance walked. simple scale-invariant, rely net displacement, unreliable convoluted random-search paths end near started. Turning-based — sinuosity() (Benhamou 2004) — built turning angles step lengths, stays meaningful net displacement small. per-observation kinematics (instantaneous speed, velocity, turning rate, plot_profile()) live main vignette — see kinematics section vignette("radiatR"). use bundled millipede dataset throughout.
code: data(cpunctatus) cpunctatus #> Tracks: 235 trajectories, 44331 observations #> Columns: id='trial_id', time='frame', angle='rel_theta' (radians), x='trans_x', y='trans_y', rel_x='rel_x', rel_y='rel_y' #> Transform steps: unit_circle_mapping  #> # A tibble: 6 × 18 #>   trial_id frame     x     y trans_x  trans_y trans_rho abs_theta rel_theta #>   <chr>    <int> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 10_1_1       1  456.  372. 0.00511  0         0.00511     0         6.21  #> 2 10_1_1       2  458.  370. 0.0128   0.00770   0.0149      0.541     0.464 #> 3 10_1_1       3  456.  370. 0.00511  0.00770   0.00924     0.985     0.908 #> 4 10_1_1       4  454.  370. 0        0.00770   0.00770     1.57      1.49  #> 5 10_1_1       5  456.  377. 0.00511 -0.0153    0.0162      5.03      4.96  #> 6 10_1_1       6  459.  382. 0.0154  -0.0307    0.0343      5.18      5.10  #> # ℹ 9 more variables: rel_x <dbl>, rel_y <dbl>, video <chr>, order <chr>, #> #   vid_ord <chr>, radius <dbl>, arc <ord>, type <chr>, individual <chr>

title: Quantifying path shape
text: track_length() total distance walked along trajectory. default unit-circle coordinates unit-circle radii; attach calibration set_distance_scale() (calibrate_distance()) reported physical units. single-path helpers path_* operate plain x/y vectors want metric one trajectory outside Tracks.

title: Quantifying path shape
text: Tracks can wander past circumference (rho > 1) – animal reaching edge, occasional tracking outlier. restrict_to_circumference() returns Tracks --circumference data removed, metrics see within-circle path. mode = "truncate" (default) keeps track first excursion beyond circumference; mode = "drop" removes individual --circumference points. data counterpart plot-radiate(clip_tracks = TRUE).
code: within <- restrict_to_circumference(cpunctatus, mode = "truncate") #> restrict_to_circumference(truncate): removed 489 of 44331 rows. head(track_length(within)) #>         trial_id    length #> 10_1_1    10_1_1 1.3645581 #> 10_10_1  10_10_1 8.7387738 #> 10_11_1  10_11_1 1.2628195 #> 10_12_1  10_12_1 1.1615569 #> 10_13_1  10_13_1 1.5757405 #> 10_14_1  10_14_1 0.9365288

title: Quantifying path shape
text: straightness index net displacement / total length, [0, 1] (1 = perfectly straight). tortuosity ratio reciprocal, [1, Inf).
code: si <- straightness_index(cpunctatus) tr <- tortuosity_ratio(cpunctatus) head(si) #>         trial_id straightness #> 10_1_1    10_1_1   0.71927750 #> 10_10_1  10_10_1   0.09897137 #> 10_11_1  10_11_1   0.79943750 #> 10_12_1  10_12_1   0.82488090 #> 10_13_1  10_13_1   0.63885806 #> 10_14_1  10_14_1   0.93581201 # single-path versions on bare coordinates path_straightness(x = c(0, 1, 2), y = c(0, 0, 0))      # straight -> 1 #> [1] 1 path_tortuosity(x = c(0, 0, 1), y = c(0, 1, 1))        # L-shaped  -> sqrt(2) #> [1] 1.414214

title: Quantifying path shape
text: sinuosity() implements Benhamou (2004) index S = 2 / sqrt(p (1 + c) / (1 - c) + b^2) (p mean step length, c mean cosine turning angles, b CV step length). straight path gives 0; winding paths give larger values. Unlike straightness index scale-invariant — units 1 / sqrt(length), reliable comparison across trajectories path sampled common step length. three tortuosity measures can read side side. join per track average within stimulus condition (arc = target half-width degrees, 0 = featureless control):
code: sn <- sinuosity(cpunctatus) head(sn) #>         trial_id sinuosity #> 10_1_1    10_1_1  3.995306 #> 10_10_1  10_10_1  2.329886 #> 10_11_1  10_11_1  3.771568 #> 10_12_1  10_12_1  3.248539 #> 10_13_1  10_13_1  3.249218 #> 10_14_1  10_14_1  1.687342 metrics <- Reduce(   function(a, b) merge(a, b, by = "trial_id"),   list(straightness_index(cpunctatus), tortuosity_ratio(cpunctatus),        sinuosity(cpunctatus)) ) arc <- unique(as.data.frame(cpunctatus)[, c("trial_id", "arc")]) metrics <- merge(metrics, arc, by = "trial_id")  aggregate(cbind(straightness, tortuosity, sinuosity) ~ arc,           data = metrics, FUN = function(v) round(mean(v, na.rm = TRUE), 2)) #>   arc straightness tortuosity sinuosity #> 1   0         0.68       2.66      2.35 #> 2   5         0.52       2.51      2.16 #> 3  10         0.57       3.36      2.46 #> 4  15         0.59       2.02      1.74 #> 5  20         0.65       1.64      1.66 #> 6  30         0.72       1.67      2.13 #> 7  40         0.75       1.39      1.95 #> 8  50         0.68       1.64      2.02

title: Quantifying path shape
text: second family spatial metrics summarises track went relative target direction — water-maze idiom (probe-trial quadrant dwell, crossings goal zone). cpunctatus natural target stimulus; landmark-relative frame stimulus sits angle 0, pass coords = "relative" target_angle = 0. count_goal_entries() counts many times track enters small goal zone target (FALSE -> TRUE crossing distance < crossing_radius): zone_dwell() gives proportion frames track spends every quadrant–ring zone (Q1 target quadrant, outer ring rim): Tune goal zone target_radius / crossing_radius annuli ring_breaks — see ?count_goal_entries ?zone_dwell.
code: head(count_goal_entries(cpunctatus, target_angle = 0, coords = "relative")) #>              id n_entries #> 10_1_1   10_1_1         0 #> 10_10_1 10_10_1         0 #> 10_11_1 10_11_1         1 #> 10_12_1 10_12_1         0 #> 10_13_1 10_13_1         1 #> 10_14_1 10_14_1         0 dwell <- zone_dwell(cpunctatus, target_angle = 0, coords = "relative") head(dwell) #>                id quadrant ring  zone n_frames proportion #> 10_1_1.1   10_1_1        1    1 Q1.R1       30 0.37037037 #> 10_1_1.2   10_1_1        2    1 Q2.R1        2 0.02469136 #> 10_1_1.3   10_1_1        4    1 Q4.R1       11 0.13580247 #> 10_1_1.4   10_1_1        1    2 Q1.R2       26 0.32098765 #> 10_1_1.5   10_1_1        1    3 Q1.R3       12 0.14814815 #> 10_10_1.1 10_10_1        1    1 Q1.R1       43 0.14381271 # mean occupancy of the target quadrant (Q1) across tracks mean(tapply(dwell$proportion[dwell$quadrant == 1],             dwell$id[dwell$quadrant == 1], sum), na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 0.5809896

title: radiatR -- Headings & Trajectories on the unit disk
text: Beyond summaries, radiatR covers common circular-statistics workflow — returning tidy data frames, accepting axial = TRUE bidirectional (mod-180°) data orientation polarization axes. Uniformity / modality tests — test_uniformity() runs Rayleigh (default), Kuiper, Rao spacing, Watson, Hermans–Rasson (Monte-Carlo, powerful multimodal alternatives) tests. Parametric fits — vonmises_fit() wrappedcauchy_fit() (axial mode), overlaid plots add_vonmises_density() / add_wrappedcauchy_density(). Model selection — circ_model_select() ranks uniform vs unimodal vs axial von Mises AICc Akaike weights, data indicate modality. Summaries & association — circ_summary() / circ_summarise(), circ_dispersion(), circ_cor() (circular–linear circular–circular). graphical app surfaces : selectable omnibus test model-selection readout alongside summary table.
code: hd <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "distal")  # Is the sample non-uniform, and how is it best described? test_uniformity(hd, test = "hermans_rasson")   # omnibus; catches multimodality circ_model_select(hd)                            # uniform / unimodal / axial by AICc

title: radiatR -- Headings & Trajectories on the unit disk
text: Model heading linear covariates (Fisher-Lee circular-linear regression):
code: # simulate a known predictor -> mean-heading effect, then recover it cond <- data.frame(condition = "demo", n_trials = 150, ref_mean = 0,                    concentration_base = 12, mean_slope = 0.6,                    predictor_mean = 0, predictor_sd = 1) s  <- simulate_tracks(conditions = cond, n_points = 8, seed = 1) hd <- s[!duplicated(s$trial_id), c("predictor", "final_heading")] names(hd)[2] <- "heading"  fit <- circ_regression(hd, heading ~ predictor) summary(fit)            # tidy coefficient table (recovers the positive slope) predict(fit, data.frame(predictor = c(-1, 0, 1)))  # draw the fitted sweep on the circular panel radiate(headings_frame(hd, heading, units = "radians")) +   add_circ_mean(fitted_directions(fit, at = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 7)),                 colour_col = "predictor")

title: radiatR -- Headings & Trajectories on the unit disk
text: Coordinates. radiatR calibrate data. default, (x, y) used exactly supplied, headings, mean direction, resultant length, circular statistics arena-accurate input coordinates. Correct lens distortion scaling real-world units tracking pipeline (e.g. tracker’s calibration, OpenCV undistort) importing. normalize_xy = TRUE available trajectory shape matters (trajectory independently centred bounding box scaled unit circle) – substitute calibration preserve bearing relative fixed arena origin. per-trial reference direction available, landmark-relative frame (rel_x/rel_y, coords = "relative" derive_headings()) accurate option.
code: library(radiatR)  # 1. Load tracking data (example: DeepLabCut with head + thorax bodyparts) ts <- read_tracks(   "my_tracks.csv",   dialect      = "deeplabcut",   dialect_args = list(bodypart = c("head", "thorax")) )  # 2. Derive headings hd <- derive_headings(ts, rule = "crossing",                       circ0 = 0.3, circ1 = 0.6,                       coords = "absolute",                       angle_convention = "unit_circle")  # 3. Circular summary per condition compute_circ_mean(hd, group_col = "condition")  # 4. Visualise radiate(ts,         group_col  = "trial_id",         colour_col = "condition",         facets     = "condition",         show_arrow = TRUE) +   add_heading_points(hd, colour_col = "condition")

title: radiatR -- Headings & Trajectories on the unit disk
text: Attach capture frame rate Tracks object time aspect frame-indexed tracks can reported real seconds shown plot. track_duration() gives elapsed seconds per trajectory, track_colour = "time" colours path elapsed time (POSIXct time works without frame rate): frame rate also lets track_speed() report trajectory speed real units (radii per second): know physical scale, calibrate distances lengths speeds report real units. scale physical units per coordinate unit (e.g. mm per radius); unset, everything stays coordinate units (radii).
code: # attach a capture rate, then represent the time aspect ts <- set_frame_rate(cpunctatus, fps = 30) track_duration(ts)                                       # seconds per track radiate(ts, show_tracks = TRUE, track_colour = "time")   # colour by elapsed time # speed in radii per second once a frame rate is set ts <- set_frame_rate(cpunctatus, fps = 30) track_speed(ts)              # mean speed per track track_speed(ts, stat = "max") # colour each path by its instantaneous speed (needs a frame rate) ts <- set_frame_rate(cpunctatus, fps = 30) radiate(ts, show_tracks = TRUE, track_colour = "speed") # calibrate distance (optional): 50 mm per coordinate unit (radius) ts <- set_distance_scale(set_frame_rate(cpunctatus, 30), 50, unit = "mm") track_length(ts)            # path length per track, in mm track_speed(ts)             # mean speed per track, in mm/s # or from two measured landmarks: # ts <- calibrate_distance(ts, coord_distance = 0.8, real_distance = 40, unit = "mm") # velocity components and turning rate (need a frame rate; vx/vy use the distance scale) ts <- set_frame_rate(cpunctatus, fps = 30) head(velocity_vector(ts))                 # vx, vy per observation head(angular_velocity(ts, units = "degrees"))   # turning rate, deg/s (CCW +) # per-track summaries (need a frame rate; velocity uses the distance scale) ts <- set_frame_rate(cpunctatus, fps = 30) track_velocity(ts)                       # net velocity vector (vx, vy) per track track_turning(ts, units = "degrees")     # typical turning rate (deg/s) per track # non-circular kinematics: speed (or turning rate) along each track over time ts <- set_frame_rate(cpunctatus, fps = 30) plot_profile(ts, metric = "speed") plot_profile(ts, metric = "turning", units = "degrees")

title: radiatR -- Headings & Trajectories on the unit disk
text: Tukey-like boxplot circular data (Buttarazzi, Pandolfo & Porzio, 2018): box spans central 50% around circular median, whiskers reach concentration-adjusted fence, far-values marked individually. Position-based axial data supported via axial = TRUE (drawn poles).
code: hd <- derive_headings(cpunctatus, rule = "crossing", circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4) radiate(cpunctatus) + add_circular_boxplot(hd)  # the summary on its own (median, hinges, fences, far-out, fence multiplier) circ_boxplot_stats(hd)$constant

title: radiatR -- Headings & Trajectories on the unit disk
text: simulate_tracks() generates synthetic circular trajectories testing pipelines teaching: condition can also carry known directional ground truth. Set modality (uniform / unimodal / axial / multimodal, n_modes) control distribution per-track headings, track shape (directed oscillatory) control within-track geometry — oscillatory tracks move back forth along axis, kind data position-based axial heading methods (pca_axis, ransac_straight) built :
code: # Three default conditions differing in concentration and tortuosity ts <- simulate_tracks(seed = 42, output = "trajset") radiate(ts, group_col = "trial_id", facets = "condition", show_arrow = TRUE)  # Custom conditions table conds <- data.frame(   condition          = c("low", "high"),   n_trials           = 20L,   concentration_base = c(1.5, 8),   tortuosity_base    = c(0.12, 0.03) ) ts2 <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds, seed = 1, output = "trajset") # Oscillatory axial tracks: each moves back-and-forth along a ~0.6 rad axis osc <- data.frame(condition = "axial_osc", n_trials = 40L, ref_mean = 0.6,                   concentration_base = 50, track_shape = "oscillatory",                   n_reversals = 4L) ts <- simulate_tracks(conditions = osc, output = "trajset", seed = 1)  # the position-based axial methods recover the ~0.6 rad axis (read as an # axial mean) at default settings: ax <- derive_headings(ts, rule = "pca_axis") circ_summarise(ax, "heading", axial = TRUE, units = "radians", stats = "mean_dir")  # a directional rule cannot: the back-and-forth directions cancel net <- derive_headings(ts, rule = "net") circ_summarise(net, "heading", units = "radians", stats = "resultant_R")

title: Add a critical resultant-length circle to a radiate plot — add_critical_r
text: Two tests supported: "rayleigh" (default) Tests uniformity     hypothesised direction.  Critical value     \(\bar R_{crit} = \sqrt{-\log(\alpha) / n}\) (asymptotic; accurate     \(n \ge 10\)). "vtest" Tests specific direction.  Critical value     \(\bar R_{crit} = z_\alpha / \sqrt{2n}\) powerful (    observed mean direction equals hypothesised \(\mu_0\));     effective threshold rises observed direction departs     \(\mu_0\), circle lower bound. Sample size n taken per occupied cell hd.  Pass column(s) facets pass radiate(rows=)/radiate(cols=) one circle drawn per panel radius appropriate panel's n.  Pass group_col split cell colour.

title: Apply a bespoke transformation to a Tracks — apply_transform
code: data(cpunctatus)  # Recipe A -- a reference-frame offset (e.g. edge-referenced -> centre- # referenced stimulus) is a frame change, so use set_reference(), not # apply_transform(): it re-derives rel_theta/rel_x/rel_y consistently. Because # rel_theta = abs - reference, offsetting the heading by +half (half the # stimulus's angular width) is a reference change of (reference - half). ts     <- set_reference(cpunctatus, 0)        # ensure a reference exists half   <- (20 / 2) * pi / 180                 # a 20-degree-wide stimulus newref <- reference(ts)$ref_theta - half      # per-trajectory if widths differ ts2    <- set_reference(ts, stats::setNames(newref, reference(ts)$id))  # Recipe B -- polarization direction -> axis. Polarized-light e-vectors are # axial (defined mod pi); doubling folds direction into orientation for axial # circular statistics. (Doubling remaps the angular space, so rel_x/rel_y are # not physical positions afterwards -- use it for the angle/stat path.) direction_to_axis <- function(df, cols) {   df[[cols$angle]] <- (2 * df[[cols$angle]]) %% (2 * pi)   df } ts_axial <- apply_transform(cpunctatus, direction_to_axis, by = "all",                             step = "direction_to_axis")

what: Select among candidate circular models by AICc — circ_model_select
title: Select among candidate circular models by AICc — circ_model_select
text: Fits five candidate models heading sample ranks small-sample-corrected Akaike information criterion (AICc): uniform preferred direction (k = 0). unimodal one von Mises mode (k = 2). axial symmetric bimodal von Mises, two equal antipodal modes (k = 2). unimodal_uniform directed von Mises mode uniform background,     \(p\,vM(\mu,\kappa) + (1-p)\,U\) (k = 3). bimodal asymmetric two-component von Mises mixture free means,     concentrations, weight (k = 5). mixture models (unimodal_uniform, bimodal) fitted numerical maximum likelihood; three closed-form. model set mirrors Schnute-Groot family implemented CircMLE package (Fitak & Johnsen 2017): uniform = M1, unimodal = single von Mises, axial = symmetric bimodal, unimodal_uniform = directed component uniform background, bimodal = free two-component mixture.

what: Sinuosity index for a single trajectory — path_sinuosity
title: Sinuosity index for a single trajectory — path_sinuosity
text: sinuosity index Benhamou (2004), turning-angle-based measure path tortuosity — unlike displacement-based [path_straightness()] / [path_tortuosity()] — rely net start--end displacement, well-behaved convoluted random-search paths: $$S = 2 \left[ p\,\frac{1+c}{1-c} + b^2 \right]^{-1/2}$$ \(p\) mean step length, \(c\) mean cosine interior turning angles, \(b\) coefficient variation step length. perfectly straight path gives `0`; winding paths give larger values.

what: radiatR: Analysis and Visualisation of Headings and Trajectories on the unit disk — radiatR-package
title: radiatR: Analysis and Visualisation of Headings and Trajectories on the unit disk — radiatR-package
text: complete pipeline analysing directional data – headings trajectories unit disk. Supply table angles directly, reconstruct headings movement trajectories imported 20+ tracking tools (EthoVision, DeepLabCut, SLEAP, TRex, -maze, TrackMate, idtracker.ai, Ctrax, others) via unified dialect-based loader system, including multi-bodypart pose estimation likelihood-weighted centroid. Derives per-trial heading directions using multiple rules (ring-crossing, distal point, pose body-axis, velocity-based, ). Computes circular statistics including mean direction, resultant length, von Mises wrapped Cauchy parametric fits, circular correlation (circular-linear circular-circular), multi-sample hypothesis tests (Watson-Williams, Rayleigh, others) multiple-comparison correction. Renders publication-quality ggplot2 radial plots overlaid rose diagrams, parametric density curves, non-parametric kernel density estimates. Includes browser-based graphical interface requiring R coding via launch_app().

title: radiatR: Analysis and Visualisation of Headings and Trajectories on the unit disk — radiatR-package

See Details column descriptions. omitted, three-condition template used. output Character string controlling return type: "tibble" (default) returns long-form data frame, "trajset" returns [Tracks] object, "" returns list containing representations. write_path Optional file path (CSV) simulated data written. seed Optional integer seed supplied [set.seed()] reproducibility. radial_noise Standard deviation radial noise applied unit radius profile. phi Autocorrelation parameter (0-1) used generating angular noise series; higher values produce smoother paths. frame_rate Optional capture frame rate (frames per second) attached returned [Tracks] via [set_frame_rate()] `output` `"trajset"` `""`. default `NULL` leaves output unchanged.

text: Depending `output`, tibble, `Tracks`, list containing   . `write_path` supplied data also written disk.

title: Simulate trajectory sets under configurable experimental conditions — simulate_tracks
text: `conditions` data frame can contain following columns (defaults supplied missing): - `condition` (character): condition label. - `n_trials` (integer): number trajectories simulate condition. - `ref_mean` (numeric radian): baseline reference heading (default 0). - `mean_slope` (numeric, default 0): per-condition slope shifting mean   heading predictor. effective per-trial mean   `ref_mean + mean_slope * predictor` recorded `ref_heading`.   default 0 reproduces historical seeded output byte--byte. - `concentration_base` (numeric): baseline von Mises concentration (kappa). - `concentration_slope` (numeric): optional slope applied predictor. - `tortuosity_base` (numeric): baseline angular noise scale. - `tortuosity_slope` (numeric): slope applied predictor. - `tortuosity_sd` (numeric): additional random variation per trial. - `predictor_mean`, `predictor_sd` (numeric): parameters used sample   per-trial predictor explicit values supplied. - `predictor_values` (list-column): optional explicit predictor values (length   `n_trials`) overriding generated values. - `modality` (character): sample modality per-trial principal   headings drawn. One `"unimodal"` (default), `"uniform"`, `"axial"`,   `"multimodal"`, `"unimodal_uniform"` (directed mode uniform   background; `mix_weight` sets directed fraction), `"bimodal"` (  asymmetric two-von-Mises mixture; `mix_weight`, `mode2_mean`, `kappa2` set   second mode). Controls *distribution headings across trials*,   within-trial path shape. - `n_modes` (integer): number evenly spaced modes used   `modality == "multimodal"` (default 1; ignored modalities). - `track_shape` (character): *within-track* path shape. One   `"directed"` (default) – single sweep towards `final_heading` –   `"oscillatory"` – back--forth motion along principal axis   `final_heading`. Oscillatory tracks form genuinely axial position cloud,   position-based axial methods (`pca_axis`, `ransac_straight`) recover   axis default settings; directional methods (e.g. `net`) cancel.   step-based `velocity_axis` recovers axis sampling   coarse enough per-step axial motion exceeds perpendicular   line-width jitter (see `line_width` note ). - `n_reversals` (integer): number direction reversals oscillatory   track (default 3; ignored `track_shape == "directed"`). - `amplitude` (numeric): peak excursion along axis oscillatory   track, clamped `[1e-3, 1]` (default 0.9; ignored directed). - `line_width` (numeric): half-width oscillatory track expressed   fraction `amplitude`, controlling perpendicular Gaussian jitter   (default 0.05, clamped `[1e-4, 1]`; ignored directed).   line-width intentionally independent `tortuosity_*`, track   genuinely thin line principal axis recoverable   position-based methods (`pca_axis`, `ransac_straight`) default settings.   step-based `velocity_axis` sampling-density sensitive:   per-frame along-axis step shrinks `n_points` perpendicular   jitter step , dense sampling lets jitter dominate   estimate flips toward perpendicular. predictor can represent continuous covariate (e.g. reference intensity). final heading concentration increases larger kappa, whereas larger tortuosity values produce sinuous paths. trial principal (final) heading drawn according condition's `modality`: `"unimodal"` draws single von Mises `ref_mean`; `"uniform"` draws circular uniform distribution; `"axial"` draws von Mises `ref_mean` `ref_mean + pi` equal probability; `"multimodal"` draws one `n_modes` von Mises components evenly spaced around circle starting `ref_mean`. `"unimodal"` branch identical historical draw, seeded output unchanged earlier versions. `"oscillatory"` track position sweeps back forth along axis `final_heading` following deterministic triangle wave amplitude `amplitude` `n_reversals` direction changes, plus small Gaussian jitter perpendicular axis standard deviation `amplitude * line_width`. line-width independent tortuosity settings, track stays thin line axis remains recoverable position-based methods (`pca_axis`, `ransac_straight`) default settings. step-based `velocity_axis` recovers axis coarse sampling, since per-step axial signal shrinks `n_points` perpendicular jitter step . `"directed"` branch byte-identical historical geometry (never draws perpendicular jitter), default seeded output unchanged. Every simulated row records ground-truth generating structure additional columns: `modality` (character), `n_modes` (integer), `mode_id` (integer index component heading came ), `mode_mean` (numeric radian mean component un-wrapped generating centre, e.g. `ref_mean + pi` second axial pole, versus `final_heading` wrapped draw; `NA` `"uniform"`), `track_shape` (character), `n_reversals` (integer), `amplitude` (numeric) `line_width` (numeric). `Tracks` returned, resolved generating conditions stored `meta$sim_conditions`.

title: Simulate trajectory sets under configurable experimental conditions — simulate_tracks
code: sim <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1) head(sim) #> # A tibble: 6 × 24 #>   condition trial_id    trial_num frame predictor concentration tortuosity #>   <chr>     <chr>           <int> <int>     <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 control   control_001         1     1    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 2 control   control_001         1     2    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 3 control   control_001         1     3    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 4 control   control_001         1     4    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 5 control   control_001         1     5    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 6 control   control_001         1     6    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> # ℹ 17 more variables: ref_heading <dbl>, final_heading <dbl>, rho <dbl>, #> #   abs_theta <dbl>, rel_theta <dbl>, abs_x <dbl>, abs_y <dbl>, rel_x <dbl>, #> #   rel_y <dbl>, modality <chr>, n_modes <int>, mode_id <int>, mode_mean <dbl>, #> #   track_shape <chr>, n_reversals <int>, amplitude <dbl>, line_width <dbl>  # Request both tibble and Tracks representations sim_both <- simulate_tracks(output = "both", seed = 123) names(sim_both) #> [1] "tibble"  "trajset"  example_conditions <- tibble::tibble(   condition = paste0("level_", 1:4),   n_trials = 30L,   ref_mean = seq(-pi/8, pi/8, length.out = 4),   concentration_base = c(2, 4, 6, 8),   concentration_slope = 0.8,   tortuosity_base = c(0.12, 0.09, 0.06, 0.04),   tortuosity_slope = -0.01,   tortuosity_sd = 0.015,   predictor_mean = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 4),   predictor_sd = 0.15 ) sim_four_levels <- simulate_tracks(   n_points = 120,   conditions = example_conditions,   output = "both",   seed = 42 ) aggregate(trial_id ~ condition, data = sim_four_levels$tibble, length) #>   condition trial_id #> 1   level_1     3600 #> 2   level_2     3600 #> 3   level_3     3600 #> 4   level_4     3600 names(sim_four_levels) #> [1] "tibble"  "trajset"

title: Test a circular sample for unimodality against bimodality — test_unimodality
text: mixture likelihood-ratio statistic non-standard null distribution (boundary / non-identifiability problem), p-value read \(\chi^2\). Instead obtained parametric bootstrap: n_boot samples simulated fitted von Mises, models refitted , observed statistic compared resulting null distribution. deliberately critical-bandwidth (Silverman) test: critical bandwidth's null distribution valid circular data (Fisher & Marron 2001), whereas bootstrap well calibrated. test computationally intensive – every bootstrap replicate refits mixture – best run modest n_boot. Set RNG seed set.seed reproducible p-values. reuses fitters behind circ_model_select, offers complementary information-criterion view unimodal / bimodal comparison.

title: Test a circular sample for unimodality against bimodality — test_unimodality

title: radiatR (development version)
text: read_tracks() read_tracks_dir() now preserve trajectory identity importing multiple files. Previously, reading character vector paths id column (common case, relying id_from_filename = TRUE) silently collapsed every file single "(multiple)" trajectory whose rows interleaved time across files. file now yields trajectory keyed file-name stem. new id_collision = c("error", "namespace") argument controls happens real id value appears one file: "error" (default) stops message naming colliding id files, "namespace" prefixes every id file stem (file::id). Multi-file imports id column id_from_filename = FALSE now fail clear error instead silent collapse, duplicate (id, time) keys produced combining files reported rather passed . read_tracks(..., time_type = "frames", fps = ) longer converts frame indices seconds twice. time column now kept raw frame index elapsed_seconds() (everything built : track_duration(), track_speed(), velocity, turning-rate) performs frame -> seconds division exactly , demand, using stored frame_rate(). Previously loader divided fps immediately stored fps frame rate, downstream kinematics divided fps second time – e.g. expected 60 units/s speed came 3600 units/s. Loading time_type = "seconds" longer stores spurious frame rate either. wrappedcauchy_fit()’s convergence column now matches documentation. circular::mle.wrappedcauchy() reports convergence logical, previous code passed .integer(), yielding 1 converged fit – opposite documented “0 = converged”. column now 0 fit converged 1 , (incorrect) “optim return code” wording docs corrected.

title: radiatR 0.1.1
text: New test_gof() tests whether sample headings fits wrapped Cauchy distribution (Watson’s U^2 probability-integral transform, parametric-bootstrap p-value), closing wrapped-Cauchy half non-von-Mises goodness--fit gap. Jones-Pewsey GOF remains deferred. test_uniformity() gains test = "pycke", Pycke (2010) omnibus uniformity test — like Hermans-Rasson, powerful multimodal asymmetric departures uniformity, Monte-Carlo p-value.

title: radiatR 0.1.1
text: radiate() longer labels every trajectory id plot many tracks. automatic fallback id/group column now applies column 12 distinct values, many-track plots (e.g. 235-trial cpunctatus) render cleanly instead buried overlapping trial-id labels. Pass label_col explicitly force labelling regardless track count. radiate(clip_tracks = TRUE) (default) now also clips beyond-circumference overshoot absolute frame (coords = "absolute"), just relative frame. relative frame rotation absolute one, rho identical ; previous relative-restriction let coords = "absolute" plots (e.g. package vignette) draw tracks visibly outside unit circle. get_all_object_pos() now reports --bounds track points (radius > 1) single aggregated message across whole manifest, instead one message per file.

title: radiatR 0.1.1
text: Summary & stats now “Group comparison” card: test_mean_directions, test_concentration, test_distributions run automatically across selected Group-column, showing whether groups differ mean direction, concentration, overall distribution (just whether individually non-uniform).

title: radiatR 0.1.0
text: First public release. Read construct movement Tracks, support absolute relative coordinate frames frame-rate / time metadata. Plot circular data radiate(), including axial overlays, faceting, grouping, perimeter labelling circular boxplots. Compute kinematics: speed, path sinuosity, per-row velocity angle related trajectory summaries. Circular statistics: circular concentration regression, model selection, tests mean direction, symmetry unimodality. Interactive Shiny app (inst/app), also deployed https://johnkirwan.shinyapps.io/radiatR/.
